message.content:
<teammate-message teammate_id="team-lead" summary="Step 5 ready for review — clarify + trash">
Step 5 (Clarify as Next Action + Trash, US-GTD-2a) is complete and CI is green. Please review from your area of expertise and send feedback to luca-palmieri.

Key files to review:
- `src/services/inbox_service.rs` — clarify_as_next_action() service function + error type
- `src/routes/inbox.rs` — post_clarify_inbox_item() handler, ClarifyForm, context passing
- `templates/inbox_item.html` — rewritten with clarify form (context select + Next Action button) and trash
- `static/css/main.css` — new clarify/trash styles
- `docs/glossary.md` — updated entries
- `tests/api/inbox.rs` — 6 new integration tests

Remember: BLOCKING / Minor / Nice-to-have categories. Check what others have flagged before writing yours.
</teammate-message>

content.text: Let me review the key files for accessibility.

1→use crate::helpers::spawn_app; 2→ 3→/// Helper: creates a reqwest client with cookie store and no redirect following. 4→fn authenticated_client() -> reqwest::Client { 5→ reqwest::Client::builder() 6→ .cookie_store(true) 7→ .redirect(reqwest::redirect::Policy::none()) 8→ .build() 9→ .unwrap() 10→} 11→ 12→/// Helper: register a user and log them in, returning the cookie-jar client. 13→async fn register_and_login(address: &str, email: &str, password: &str) -> reqwest::Client { 14→ let client = authenticated_client(); 15→ 16→ // Register 17→ let resp = client 18→ .post(format!("{address}/register")) 19→ .form(&[ 20→ ("email", email), 21→ ("password", password), 22→ ("password_confirmation", password), 23→ ]) 24→ .send() 25→ .await 26→ .expect("Failed to register"); 27→ assert_eq!(resp.status().as_u16(), 303, "Registration should redirect"); 28→ 29→ // Login 30→ let resp = client 31→ .post(format!("{address}/login")) 32→ .form(&[("email", email), ("password", password)]) 33→ .send() 34→ .await 35→ .expect("Failed to login"); 36→ assert_eq!(resp.status().as_u16(), 303, "Login should redirect"); 37→ 38→ client 39→} 40→ 41→// ---- Capture Tests ---- 42→ 43→#[tokio::test] 44→async fn post_inbox_captures_item_and_redirects() { 45→ let app = spawn_app().await; 46→ let client = register_and_login(&app.address, "capture@example.com", "securepassword123").await; 47→ 48→ let response = client 49→ .post(format!("{}/inbox", &app.address)) 50→ .form(&[("title", "Buy milk")]) 51→ .send() 52→ .await 53→ .expect("Failed to post to inbox"); 54→ 55→ assert_eq!( 56→ response.status().as_u16(), 57→ 303, 58→ "POST /inbox should redirect" 59→ ); 60→ 61→ let location = response 62→ .headers() 63→ .get("location") 64→ .expect("Missing Location header") 65→ .to_str() 66→ .unwrap(); 67→ assert_eq!(location, "/inbox", "Should redirect to /inbox"); 68→ 69→ // Verify the item shows on the inbox page 70→ let response = client 71→ .get(format!("{}/inbox", &app.address)) 72→ .send() 73→ .await 74→ .expect("Failed to GET /inbox"); 75→ 76→ let body = response.text().await.unwrap(); 77→ assert!( 78→ body.contains("Buy milk"), 79→ "Inbox page should show the captured item" 80→ ); 81→} 82→ 83→#[tokio::test] 84→async fn post_inbox_with_empty_title_silently_redirects() { 85→ let app = spawn_app().await; 86→ let client = register_and_login(&app.address, "empty@example.com", "securepassword123").await; 87→ 88→ let response = client 89→ .post(format!("{}/inbox", &app.address)) 90→ .form(&[("title", "")]) 91→ .send() 92→ .await 93→ .expect("Failed to post to inbox"); 94→ 95→ assert_eq!( 96→ response.status().as_u16(), 97→ 303, 98→ "Empty title should silently redirect" 99→ ); 100→} 101→ 102→#[tokio::test] 103→async fn post_inbox_with_whitespace_only_title_silently_redirects() { 104→ let app = spawn_app().await; 105→ let client = 106→ register_and_login(&app.address, "whitespace@example.com", "securepassword123").await; 107→ 108→ let response = client 109→ .post(format!("{}/inbox", &app.address)) 110→ .form(&[("title", " ")]) 111→ .send() 112→ .await 113→ .expect("Failed to post to inbox"); 114→ 115→ assert_eq!( 116→ response.status().as_u16(), 117→ 303, 118→ "Whitespace-only title should silently redirect" 119→ ); 120→} 121→ 122→#[tokio::test] 123→async fn post_inbox_with_too_long_title_returns_422() { 124→ let app = spawn_app().await; 125→ let client = register_and_login(&app.address, "toolong@example.com", "securepassword123").await; 126→ 127→ let long_title = "a".repeat(301); 128→ let response = client 129→ .post(format!("{}/inbox", &app.address)) 130→ .form(&[("title", long_title.as_str())]) 131→ .send() 132→ .await 133→ .expect("Failed to post to inbox"); 134→ 135→ assert_eq!( 136→ response.status().as_u16(), 137→ 422, 138→ "Too-long title should return 422" 139→ ); 140→} 141→ 142→// ---- Empty State ---- 143→ 144→#[tokio::test] 145→async fn inbox_shows_empty_state_when_no_items() { 146→ let app = spawn_app().await; 147→ let client = 148→ register_and_login(&app.address, "emptyinbox@example.com", "securepassword123").await; 149→ 150→ let response = client 151→ .get(format!("{}/inbox", &app.address)) 152→ .send() 153→ .await 154→ .expect("Failed to GET /inbox"); 155→ 156→ let body = response.text().await.unwrap(); 157→ assert!( 158→ body.contains("Inbox zero"), 159→ "Empty inbox should show 'Inbox zero' message" 160→ ); 161→ assert!( 162→ body.contains("weekly review"), 163→ "Empty state should mention weekly review" 164→ ); 165→} 166→ 167→// ---- Delete Tests ---- 168→ 169→#[tokio::test] 170→async fn delete_inbox_item_removes_it_and_redirects() { 171→ let app = spawn_app().await; 172→ let client = register_and_login(&app.address, "delete@example.com", "securepassword123").await; 173→ 174→ // Capture an item 175→ client 176→ .post(format!("{}/inbox", &app.address)) 177→ .form(&[("title", "Delete me")]) 178→ .send() 179→ .await 180→ .expect("Failed to capture"); 181→ 182→ // Get inbox page to find the item's ID from the delete form action 183→ let response = client 184→ .get(format!("{}/inbox", &app.address)) 185→ .send() 186→ .await 187→ .expect("Failed to GET /inbox"); 188→ 189→ let body = response.text().await.unwrap(); 190→ assert!(body.contains("Delete me")); 191→ 192→ // Extract the item ID from the delete form action URL 193→ let delete_action_prefix = "action=\"/inbox/"; 194→ // Find the delete form action specifically (contains /delete") 195→ let delete_action_pos = body.find("/delete\"").expect("Missing /delete action"); 196→ // Walk backwards to find the start of the action attribute value 197→ let action_start = body[..delete_action_pos] 198→ .rfind(delete_action_prefix) 199→ .expect("Missing delete form action prefix") 200→ + delete_action_prefix.len(); 201→ let item_id = &body[action_start..delete_action_pos]; 202→ 203→ // Delete the item 204→ let response = client 205→ .post(format!("{}/inbox/{}/delete", &app.address, item_id)) 206→ .send() 207→ .await 208→ .expect("Failed to delete"); 209→ 210→ assert_eq!(response.status().as_u16(), 303, "DELETE should redirect"); 211→ 212→ // Verify item is gone 213→ let response = client 214→ .get(format!("{}/inbox", &app.address)) 215→ .send() 216→ .await 217→ .expect("Failed to GET /inbox"); 218→ 219→ let body = response.text().await.unwrap(); 220→ assert!( 221→ !body.contains("Delete me"), 222→ "Deleted item should no longer appear" 223→ ); 224→} 225→ 226→#[tokio::test] 227→async fn delete_nonexistent_inbox_item_returns_404() { 228→ let app = spawn_app().await; 229→ let client = register_and_login(&app.address, "del404@example.com", "securepassword123").await; 230→ 231→ let fake_id = uuid::Uuid::new_v4(); 232→ let response = client 233→ .post(format!("{}/inbox/{}/delete", &app.address, fake_id)) 234→ .send() 235→ .await 236→ .expect("Failed to delete"); 237→ 238→ assert_eq!(response.status().as_u16(), 404); 239→} 240→ 241→// ---- User Isolation ---- 242→ 243→#[tokio::test] 244→async fn users_cannot_see_each_others_inbox_items() { 245→ let app = spawn_app().await; 246→ 247→ let client_a = 248→ register_and_login(&app.address, "user_a@example.com", "securepassword123").await; 249→ let client_b = 250→ register_and_login(&app.address, "user_b@example.com", "securepassword123").await; 251→ 252→ // User A captures an item 253→ client_a 254→ .post(format!("{}/inbox", &app.address)) 255→ .form(&[("title", "User A secret")]) 256→ .send() 257→ .await 258→ .expect("Failed to capture"); 259→ 260→ // User B should not see it 261→ let response = client_b 262→ .get(format!("{}/inbox", &app.address)) 263→ .send() 264→ .await 265→ .expect("Failed to GET /inbox"); 266→ 267→ let body = response.text().await.unwrap(); 268→ assert!( 269→ !body.contains("User A secret"), 270→ "User B should not see User A's inbox items" 271→ ); 272→} 273→ 274→// ---- Inbox Count Badge ---- 275→ 276→#[tokio::test] 277→async fn inbox_count_shows_in_dashboard_after_capture() { 278→ let app = spawn_app().await; 279→ let client = register_and_login(&app.address, "count@example.com", "securepassword123").await; 280→ 281→ // Capture two items 282→ client 283→ .post(format!("{}/inbox", &app.address)) 284→ .form(&[("title", "Item one")]) 285→ .send() 286→ .await 287→ .expect("Failed to capture"); 288→ client 289→ .post(format!("{}/inbox", &app.address)) 290→ .form(&[("title", "Item two")]) 291→ .send() 292→ .await 293→ .expect("Failed to capture"); 294→ 295→ // Dashboard should show count of 2 296→ let response = client 297→ .get(format!("{}/dashboard", &app.address)) 298→ .send() 299→ .await 300→ .expect("Failed to GET /dashboard"); 301→ 302→ let body = response.text().await.unwrap(); 303→ assert!( 304→ body.contains("2 items"), 305→ "Dashboard should show '2 items' for inbox count" 306→ ); 307→} 308→ 309→#[tokio::test] 310→async fn inbox_nav_badge_shows_count() { 311→ let app = spawn_app().await; 312→ let client = register_and_login(&app.address, "badge@example.com", "securepassword123").await; 313→ 314→ // Capture an item 315→ client 316→ .post(format!("{}/inbox", &app.address)) 317→ .form(&[("title", "Badge test")]) 318→ .send() 319→ .await 320→ .expect("Failed to capture"); 321→ 322→ // Check that the nav shows the badge with count 323→ let response = client 324→ .get(format!("{}/inbox", &app.address)) 325→ .send() 326→ .await 327→ .expect("Failed to GET /inbox"); 328→ 329→ let body = response.text().await.unwrap(); 330→ assert!( 331→ body.contains("gtd-nav__badge"), 332→ "Nav should show inbox count badge when items exist" 333→ ); 334→} 335→ 336→// ---- Accessibility ---- 337→ 338→#[tokio::test] 339→async fn inbox_page_has_visible_label_for_capture_input() { 340→ let app = spawn_app().await; 341→ let client = register_and_login(&app.address, "a11y@example.com", "securepassword123").await; 342→ 343→ let response = client 344→ .get(format!("{}/inbox", &app.address)) 345→ .send() 346→ .await 347→ .expect("Failed to GET /inbox"); 348→ 349→ let body = response.text().await.unwrap(); 350→ assert!( 351→ body.contains("" 353→ ); 354→ assert!( 355→ body.contains("for=\"inbox-title\""), 356→ "Label should be associated with capture input via for/id" 357→ ); 358→} 359→ 360→#[tokio::test] 361→async fn inbox_page_has_proper_heading() { 362→ let app = spawn_app().await; 363→ let client = register_and_login(&app.address, "heading@example.com", "securepassword123").await; 364→ 365→ let response = client 366→ .get(format!("{}/inbox", &app.address)) 367→ .send() 368→ .await 369→ .expect("Failed to GET /inbox"); 370→ 371→ let body = response.text().await.unwrap(); 372→ assert!( 373→ body.contains("

Inbox

"), 374→ "Inbox page should have

Inbox

" 375→ ); 376→} 377→ 378→#[tokio::test] 379→async fn delete_button_has_accessible_label_with_item_title() { 380→ let app = spawn_app().await; 381→ let client = 382→ register_and_login(&app.address, "dellabel@example.com", "securepassword123").await; 383→ 384→ // Capture an item 385→ client 386→ .post(format!("{}/inbox", &app.address)) 387→ .form(&[("title", "Call the dentist")]) 388→ .send() 389→ .await 390→ .expect("Failed to capture"); 391→ 392→ let response = client 393→ .get(format!("{}/inbox", &app.address)) 394→ .send() 395→ .await 396→ .expect("Failed to GET /inbox"); 397→ 398→ let body = response.text().await.unwrap(); 399→ assert!( 400→ body.contains("aria-label=\"Trash: Call the dentist\""), 401→ "Trash button should have aria-label including the item title" 402→ ); 403→} 404→ 405→// ---- HTMX Fragment Responses ---- 406→ 407→#[tokio::test] 408→async fn htmx_post_inbox_returns_fragment_with_announce_header() { 409→ let app = spawn_app().await; 410→ let client = register_and_login(&app.address, "htmx@example.com", "securepassword123").await; 411→ 412→ let response = client 413→ .post(format!("{}/inbox", &app.address)) 414→ .header("hx-request", "true") 415→ .form(&[("title", "HTMX capture")]) 416→ .send() 417→ .await 418→ .expect("Failed to post to inbox"); 419→ 420→ assert_eq!( 421→ response.status().as_u16(), 422→ 200, 423→ "HTMX POST should return 200 with fragment" 424→ ); 425→ 426→ // Should have HX-Trigger header with announce event 427→ let trigger = response 428→ .headers() 429→ .get("hx-trigger") 430→ .expect("Missing HX-Trigger header") 431→ .to_str() 432→ .unwrap(); 433→ assert!( 434→ trigger.contains("Captured to inbox"), 435→ "HX-Trigger should contain 'Captured to inbox' announce" 436→ ); 437→ 438→ let body = response.text().await.unwrap(); 439→ assert!( 440→ body.contains("HTMX capture"), 441→ "Fragment should contain the captured item" 442→ ); 443→} 444→ 445→#[tokio::test] 446→async fn htmx_post_inbox_with_empty_title_returns_204() { 447→ let app = spawn_app().await; 448→ let client = 449→ register_and_login(&app.address, "htmxempty@example.com", "securepassword123").await; 450→ 451→ let response = client 452→ .post(format!("{}/inbox", &app.address)) 453→ .header("hx-request", "true") 454→ .form(&[("title", "")]) 455→ .send() 456→ .await 457→ .expect("Failed to post to inbox"); 458→ 459→ assert_eq!( 460→ response.status().as_u16(), 461→ 204, 462→ "HTMX POST with empty title should return 204" 463→ ); 464→} 465→ 466→#[tokio::test] 467→async fn htmx_delete_inbox_item_returns_empty_body_with_announce() { 468→ let app = spawn_app().await; 469→ let client = register_and_login(&app.address, "htmxdel@example.com", "securepassword123").await; 470→ 471→ // Capture an item 472→ client 473→ .post(format!("{}/inbox", &app.address)) 474→ .form(&[("title", "HTMX delete test")]) 475→ .send() 476→ .await 477→ .expect("Failed to capture"); 478→ 479→ // Get inbox to find item ID 480→ let response = client 481→ .get(format!("{}/inbox", &app.address)) 482→ .send() 483→ .await 484→ .expect("Failed to GET /inbox"); 485→ let body = response.text().await.unwrap(); 486→ 487→ let delete_action_prefix = "action=\"/inbox/"; 488→ let delete_action_pos = body.find("/delete\"").expect("Missing /delete action"); 489→ let action_start = body[..delete_action_pos] 490→ .rfind(delete_action_prefix) 491→ .expect("Missing delete form action prefix") 492→ + delete_action_prefix.len(); 493→ let item_id = &body[action_start..delete_action_pos]; 494→ 495→ // Delete via HTMX 496→ let response = client 497→ .post(format!("{}/inbox/{}/delete", &app.address, item_id)) 498→ .header("hx-request", "true") 499→ .send() 500→ .await 501→ .expect("Failed to delete"); 502→ 503→ assert_eq!(response.status().as_u16(), 200); 504→ 505→ let trigger = response 506→ .headers() 507→ .get("hx-trigger") 508→ .expect("Missing HX-Trigger header") 509→ .to_str() 510→ .unwrap(); 511→ assert!( 512→ trigger.contains("Inbox item deleted"), 513→ "HX-Trigger should contain 'Inbox item deleted' announce" 514→ ); 515→ 516→ let body = response.text().await.unwrap(); 517→ assert!(body.is_empty(), "HTMX delete should return empty body"); 518→} 519→ 520→// ---- Quick Capture From Header ---- 521→ 522→#[tokio::test] 523→async fn quick_capture_form_posts_to_inbox() { 524→ let app = spawn_app().await; 525→ let client = 526→ register_and_login(&app.address, "quickcap@example.com", "securepassword123").await; 527→ 528→ // Verify the quick capture form on the dashboard page 529→ let response = client 530→ .get(format!("{}/dashboard", &app.address)) 531→ .send() 532→ .await 533→ .expect("Failed to GET /dashboard"); 534→ 535→ let body = response.text().await.unwrap(); 536→ assert!( 537→ body.contains("action=\"/inbox\""), 538→ "Quick capture form on dashboard should post to /inbox" 539→ ); 540→ 541→ // Post via quick capture (same endpoint) 542→ let response = client 543→ .post(format!("{}/inbox", &app.address)) 544→ .form(&[("title", "From quick capture")]) 545→ .send() 546→ .await 547→ .expect("Failed to post"); 548→ 549→ assert_eq!(response.status().as_u16(), 303); 550→ 551→ // Item should appear in inbox 552→ let response = client 553→ .get(format!("{}/inbox", &app.address)) 554→ .send() 555→ .await 556→ .expect("Failed to GET /inbox"); 557→ 558→ let body = response.text().await.unwrap(); 559→ assert!( 560→ body.contains("From quick capture"), 561→ "Item captured via quick capture should appear in inbox" 562→ ); 563→} 564→ 565→/// Helper: get the first context ID from the contexts page 566→async fn get_first_context_id(client: &reqwest::Client, address: &str) -> String { 567→ let response = client 568→ .get(format!("{address}/contexts")) 569→ .send() 570→ .await 571→ .expect("Failed to GET /contexts"); 572→ 573→ let body = response.text().await.unwrap(); 574→ let action_prefix = "action=\"/contexts/"; 575→ let start = body.find(action_prefix).expect("Missing context action") + action_prefix.len(); 576→ let end = body[start..].find('/').expect("Missing / after context ID"); 577→ body[start..start + end].to_string() 578→} 579→ 580→/// Helper: get the first inbox item ID from the inbox page 581→async fn get_first_inbox_item_id(client: &reqwest::Client, address: &str) -> String { 582→ let response = client 583→ .get(format!("{address}/inbox")) 584→ .send() 585→ .await 586→ .expect("Failed to GET /inbox"); 587→ 588→ let body = response.text().await.unwrap(); 589→ let action_prefix = "action=\"/inbox/"; 590→ let start = body.find(action_prefix).expect("Missing inbox item action") + action_prefix.len(); 591→ let end = body[start..].find('/').expect("Missing / after item ID"); 592→ body[start..start + end].to_string() 593→} 594→ 595→// ---- Clarify as Next Action ---- 596→ 597→#[tokio::test] 598→async fn clarify_inbox_item_as_next_action_removes_from_inbox() { 599→ let app = spawn_app().await; 600→ let client = 601→ register_and_login(&app.address, "clarify1@example.com", "securepassword123").await; 602→ 603→ let context_id = get_first_context_id(&client, &app.address).await; 604→ 605→ // Capture an item 606→ client 607→ .post(format!("{}/inbox", &app.address)) 608→ .form(&[("title", "Clarify me")]) 609→ .send() 610→ .await 611→ .expect("Failed to capture"); 612→ 613→ let item_id = get_first_inbox_item_id(&client, &app.address).await; 614→ 615→ // Clarify as next action 616→ let response = client 617→ .post(format!("{}/inbox/{}/clarify", &app.address, item_id)) 618→ .form(&[("context_id", &context_id)]) 619→ .send() 620→ .await 621→ .expect("Failed to clarify"); 622→ 623→ assert_eq!(response.status().as_u16(), 303, "Clarify should redirect"); 624→ 625→ // Item should be gone from inbox 626→ let response = client 627→ .get(format!("{}/inbox", &app.address)) 628→ .send() 629→ .await 630→ .expect("Failed to GET /inbox"); 631→ let body = response.text().await.unwrap(); 632→ assert!( 633→ !body.contains("Clarify me"), 634→ "Clarified item should no longer appear in inbox" 635→ ); 636→} 637→ 638→#[tokio::test] 639→async fn clarify_inbox_item_creates_next_action() { 640→ let app = spawn_app().await; 641→ let client = 642→ register_and_login(&app.address, "clarify2@example.com", "securepassword123").await; 643→ 644→ let context_id = get_first_context_id(&client, &app.address).await; 645→ 646→ // Capture an item 647→ client 648→ .post(format!("{}/inbox", &app.address)) 649→ .form(&[("title", "Becomes next action")]) 650→ .send() 651→ .await 652→ .expect("Failed to capture"); 653→ 654→ let item_id = get_first_inbox_item_id(&client, &app.address).await; 655→ 656→ // Clarify as next action 657→ client 658→ .post(format!("{}/inbox/{}/clarify", &app.address, item_id)) 659→ .form(&[("context_id", &context_id)]) 660→ .send() 661→ .await 662→ .expect("Failed to clarify"); 663→ 664→ // Item should appear in next actions 665→ let response = client 666→ .get(format!("{}/next-actions", &app.address)) 667→ .send() 668→ .await 669→ .expect("Failed to GET /next-actions"); 670→ let body = response.text().await.unwrap(); 671→ assert!( 672→ body.contains("Becomes next action"), 673→ "Clarified item should appear in next actions list" 674→ ); 675→} 676→ 677→#[tokio::test] 678→async fn clarify_nonexistent_inbox_item_returns_404() { 679→ let app = spawn_app().await; 680→ let client = 681→ register_and_login(&app.address, "clarify3@example.com", "securepassword123").await; 682→ 683→ let context_id = get_first_context_id(&client, &app.address).await; 684→ let fake_id = uuid::Uuid::new_v4(); 685→ 686→ let response = client 687→ .post(format!("{}/inbox/{}/clarify", &app.address, fake_id)) 688→ .form(&[("context_id", &context_id)]) 689→ .send() 690→ .await 691→ .expect("Failed to clarify"); 692→ 693→ assert_eq!(response.status().as_u16(), 404); 694→} 695→ 696→#[tokio::test] 697→async fn htmx_clarify_returns_empty_body_with_announce() { 698→ let app = spawn_app().await; 699→ let client = 700→ register_and_login(&app.address, "clarify4@example.com", "securepassword123").await; 701→ 702→ let context_id = get_first_context_id(&client, &app.address).await; 703→ 704→ client 705→ .post(format!("{}/inbox", &app.address)) 706→ .form(&[("title", "HTMX clarify test")]) 707→ .send() 708→ .await 709→ .expect("Failed to capture"); 710→ 711→ let item_id = get_first_inbox_item_id(&client, &app.address).await; 712→ 713→ let response = client 714→ .post(format!("{}/inbox/{}/clarify", &app.address, item_id)) 715→ .header("hx-request", "true") 716→ .form(&[("context_id", &context_id)]) 717→ .send() 718→ .await 719→ .expect("Failed to clarify"); 720→ 721→ assert_eq!(response.status().as_u16(), 200); 722→ 723→ let trigger = response 724→ .headers() 725→ .get("hx-trigger") 726→ .expect("Missing HX-Trigger") 727→ .to_str() 728→ .unwrap(); 729→ assert!( 730→ trigger.contains("Clarified as next action"), 731→ "HX-Trigger should contain announce" 732→ ); 733→ 734→ let body = response.text().await.unwrap(); 735→ assert!( 736→ body.is_empty(), 737→ "HTMX clarify should return empty body (item removed)" 738→ ); 739→} 740→ 741→#[tokio::test] 742→async fn inbox_item_has_clarify_form_with_context_select() { 743→ let app = spawn_app().await; 744→ let client = 745→ register_and_login(&app.address, "clarify5@example.com", "securepassword123").await; 746→ 747→ client 748→ .post(format!("{}/inbox", &app.address)) 749→ .form(&[("title", "Item with clarify")]) 750→ .send() 751→ .await 752→ .expect("Failed to capture"); 753→ 754→ let response = client 755→ .get(format!("{}/inbox", &app.address)) 756→ .send() 757→ .await 758→ .expect("Failed to GET /inbox"); 759→ let body = response.text().await.unwrap(); 760→ 761→ assert!( 762→ body.contains("/clarify"), 763→ "Inbox item should have a clarify form action" 764→ ); 765→ assert!( 766→ body.contains("Next Action"), 767→ "Inbox item should have a 'Next Action' clarify button" 768→ ); 769→ assert!( 770→ body.contains("Trash"), 771→ "Inbox item should have a 'Trash' button" 772→ ); 773→ assert!( 774→ body.contains("inbox-item__context-select"), 775→ "Inbox item should have a context select for clarifying" 776→ ); 777→} 778→ 779→#[tokio::test] 780→async fn inbox_item_clarify_button_has_accessible_label() { 781→ let app = spawn_app().await; 782→ let client = 783→ register_and_login(&app.address, "clarify6@example.com", "securepassword123").await; 784→ 785→ client 786→ .post(format!("{}/inbox", &app.address)) 787→ .form(&[("title", "A11y clarify test")]) 788→ .send() 789→ .await 790→ .expect("Failed to capture"); 791→ 792→ let response = client 793→ .get(format!("{}/inbox", &app.address)) 794→ .send() 795→ .await 796→ .expect("Failed to GET /inbox"); 797→ let body = response.text().await.unwrap(); 798→ 799→ assert!( 800→ body.contains("aria-label=\"Clarify as next action: A11y clarify test\""), 801→ "Clarify button should have aria-label including item title" 802→ ); 803→ assert!( 804→ body.contains("aria-label=\"Trash: A11y clarify test\""), 805→ "Trash button should have aria-label including item title" 806→ ); 807→} 808→

content.text: Now let me check whether there's inbox-specific focus management for the clarify and trash operations (since both remove items from the DOM via HTMX).
Let me also verify the CSS for the clarify button's hover state and check whether the context label has adequate contrast.
I have a complete picture now. Let me send my review.

input.content:
Heydon Pickering -- Accessibility Review for Step 5: Clarify as Next Action + Trash

Good work applying the lessons from Step 4 on accessible names and visible labels. Here is my full review.

## What's good (no changes needed)

- **Accessible names on buttons**: `aria-label="Clarify as next action: {{ item.title }}"` and `aria-label="Trash: {{ item.title }}"` -- both include the item title, exactly right. Integration tests verify both (lines 780-807 of tests/api/inbox.rs).
- **Visible label on context select**: `<label for="clarify-context-{{ item.id }}" class="inbox-item__context-label">Context</label>` with a properly unique `id` per item. You learned from Step 4 -- good.
- **Live region announcements**: "Clarified as next action" and "Inbox item deleted" via `htmx_response_with_announce`. Correct.
- **Progressive enhancement**: Both clarify and trash forms work without JS via standard POST/redirect. Correct.
- **Button labels**: "Next Action" and "Trash" are clear, descriptive action labels (not "Submit" or "OK"). Good copy.
- **Empty state**: "Inbox zero" message with helpful links to capture and weekly review. Good empty state guidance.
- **Semantic structure**: Items in a `<ul role="list">`, proper `<h1>`, landmark regions from base template all inherited correctly.

## BLOCKING Issues

**1. Focus management after clarify/trash -- missing (same issue as Step 4)**

When an inbox item is clarified or trashed via HTMX, the `<li>` is removed (`hx-swap="outerHTML"` with empty body). Focus falls to `<body>`. This is the same issue we fixed for next actions with `next-action-focus.js`.

The inbox page (`templates/inbox.html` lines 48-68) has inline scripts for empty state toggling but no focus management.

**Fix**: Create `static/js/inbox-focus.js` (or add to the existing inline scripts) following the same pattern as `next-action-focus.js`:
- On `htmx:beforeSwap` targeting `.inbox-item`, if the response is empty, capture next/previous sibling `.inbox-item`
- On `htmx:afterSettle`, focus the captured sibling, or `#inbox-title` if the list is now empty
- Load via `<script src="/static/js/inbox-focus.js"></script>` in the scripts block

This is the third time we've needed this pattern (todo, next actions, inbox). After this fix, I'd suggest the team consider extracting a reusable focus management helper.

## Minor Issues

**2. Trash button does not have a confirmation step**

The old todo delete used `<details>`/`<summary>` for two-click delete. The inbox trash is a single-click destructive action. While this is less critical for inbox items (they're unclarified raw captures, not processed work), it's still a data loss action.

I'm marking this **Minor** rather than blocking because:
- Inbox items are by nature quick captures, not refined work
- The button label "Trash" is clear about what it does
- The no-JS fallback redirects to /inbox which provides visual feedback

If the team prefers to add a confirmation, the `<details>`/`<summary>` pattern from the old todo list works well. But I won't block on this.

**3. The clarify button hover uses `opacity: 0.9` instead of a color change**

The clarify button hover (CSS line 1245-1247) uses `opacity: 0.9` which is barely perceptible. The existing pattern for primary buttons uses `background-color: var(--color-primary-dark)` on hover. This should match for consistency and to provide a visible hover feedback.

## Nice-to-have

**4. Consider grouping the clarify controls with a wrapping description**

The clarify form has a context select + "Next Action" button. For screen readers, these are just two controls inside a form. It might be slightly clearer if the form had an `aria-label` like `"Clarify as next action"` on the `<form>` element, so users navigating by landmarks/forms understand the form's purpose without reading each control.

Not blocking because the individual controls are properly labeled and the button text "Next Action" is descriptive.

---

**Summary**: One BLOCKING item (focus management -- the same pattern we keep needing). Two minor items (trash confirmation, hover opacity). One nice-to-have. The accessible names, visible labels, live region announcements, and progressive enhancement are all correct from the initial implementation -- good improvement from Step 4.
input.summary: A11y review: 1 BLOCKING (focus mgmt), 2 minor, 1 nice-to-have

**4. Consider grouping the clarify controls with a wrapping description**

input.content:
Accessibility review for Step 5 complete, sent to Luca. Summary:

**1 BLOCKING**: Focus management after clarify/trash -- same pattern as Step 4. When inbox items are removed via HTMX, focus falls to body. Needs an `inbox-focus.js` following the `next-action-focus.js` pattern. This is the third time we've hit this (todo, next actions, inbox). I've suggested the team consider extracting a reusable focus management helper after this fix.

**2 Minor**: (1) Trash has no confirmation step (acceptable for inbox items, not blocking). (2) Clarify button hover uses `opacity: 0.9` instead of `color-primary-dark` -- inconsistent with other buttons.

**1 Nice-to-have**: Form-level `aria-label` on the clarify form.

Good news: Accessible names, visible labels, and live region announcements were all correct from the initial implementation. The retro feedback from Step 4 clearly helped.

content.text:
My accessibility review for Step 5 is complete. 

**1 BLOCKING**: Focus management after clarify/trash -- the inbox page is missing the same focus management pattern we've now implemented three times (todo list, next actions, and now needed for inbox). When items are removed via HTMX, focus falls to `<body>`.

**2 Minor**: Trash lacks a confirmation step (acceptable for raw captures); clarify button hover uses `opacity: 0.9` instead of `color-primary-dark`.

**1 Nice-to-have**: Form-level `aria-label` on the clarify form.

Positive note: The accessible names (`"Clarify as next action: ..."`, `"Trash: ..."`), visible labels on the context select, and live region announcements were all correct from the initial commit. The Step 4 retro feedback about treating the a11y DoD checklist as a pre-commit gate is working for those items. Focus management remains the recurring gap.
